profile.name: Rakshit Jain
profile.title: Product Manager | Supply Chain Technology
profile.tagline: Building WMS, TMS, AI Automation and Digital Supply Chain Solutions

projects.title: JAWA
projects.description: End-to-end WMS implementation across multi-site warehouse operations, driving inventory accuracy and throughput improvements.
projects.writeup:
JAWA is a full-scale Warehouse Management System implementation project spanning multiple distribution centres. The goal was to replace manual, paper-based processes with a digitised, real-time inventory tracking system.

Key outcomes:
• Reduced inventory discrepancy from 4.2% to 0.3% within 3 months of go-live
• Improved pick-pack-ship cycle time by 38% through optimised put-away and wave planning logic
• Integrated barcode scanning and IoT weight sensors at inbound docks for automated GRN creation
• Designed custom exception workflows for damaged goods, short shipments and quarantine holds

I led the product side — defining business requirements, coordinating with the WMS vendor, running UAT, and managing the change management programme with warehouse staff.

projects.title: VisionWare
projects.description: Interactive 3D warehouse layout analytics platform for real-time slotting optimisation and space utilisation visibility.
projects.writeup:
VisionWare is an in-house analytics tool that renders a warehouse's physical layout in an interactive 3D view, overlaid with live data from the WMS.

What it does:
• Visualises bin-level occupancy, velocity class, and pick frequency on a 3D floor plan
• Flags slotting inefficiencies — high-velocity SKUs stored far from despatch, dead stock occupying prime locations
• Simulates proposed slotting changes and projects the impact on travel distance before committing
• Exports heat maps and PDF reports for operations review meetings

Built with Three.js on the frontend and a FastAPI backend that pipes data from the WMS database. I defined the product requirements and worked directly with one developer to ship an MVP in 6 weeks.

projects.title: Apollo Control Tower
projects.description: Real-time supply chain visibility dashboard aggregating shipment, inventory and carrier data across the network.
projects.writeup:
Apollo Control Tower is an internal visibility platform built to give the operations and planning teams a single pane of glass across inbound, outbound and inter-branch shipments.

Core features:
• Live shipment tracking with ETA predictions using carrier API integrations
• Automated exception alerts for delayed shipments, SLA breaches and carrier non-performance
• Inventory-in-transit view reconciled against purchase orders and sales orders
• AI-powered delay risk scoring that flags at-risk shipments 24–48 hours before breach
• Role-based dashboards for planners, ops managers and the C-suite

Deployed on AWS using Lambda for event-driven alert processing. I owned the full product lifecycle from discovery through to adoption metrics tracking.

projects.title: E-commerce Platform
projects.description: Full-featured e-commerce platform with payments integration, order management and warehouse fulfilment connectivity.
projects.writeup:
A ground-up e-commerce platform built to connect online storefronts directly to warehouse fulfilment systems — bridging the gap between customer order and physical despatch.

Highlights:
• Stripe-powered checkout with support for split payments, refunds and partial fulfilment
• Real-time stock availability synced from the WMS to prevent overselling
• Order routing logic that assigns fulfilment to the nearest stocked warehouse location
• Returns management portal with automated RMA generation and stock reconciliation
• Admin dashboard for catalogue management, pricing rules and order operations

Built as a React SPA with a Node.js API layer. The WMS integration was the most complex piece — translating e-commerce order lines into WMS pick tasks while handling partial stock and backorder scenarios.

experience.description: Building scalable applications using React, Python and cloud technologies.
Worked on automation systems, AI integrations and AWS deployments.
Explored frontend, backend, databases, APIs and modern web architecture.
